merger_name: AXDI - minority interest in La Trobe Financial
merger_description:
AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (**AXDI**) proposes to acquire a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd (the **Target**) from BCP AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP LLC) (**Brookfield**), by way of a share sale agreement.

AXDI is a newly incorporated holding entity managed by Axight Capital Limited (**Axight**), that was established for the sole purpose of acquiring an interest in the issued share capital of the Target pursuant to the acquisition.

The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets under management, specialising in finance and credit investment solutions. La Trobe Financial operates across the following aspects of the wealth value chain:

* investment management and product manufacturing (managers investing client capital and manufacturers creating products to be acquired),
* platforms (technology platforms that allow advisers to manage clients' portfolios), and
* distribution (professional financial advisers who provide tailored advice to individuals and execute investment decisions).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (AXDI) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd
(the holding company of La Trobe Financial) (the Target) from BCP
AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP
LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP
LLC) (Brookfield), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AXDI, is a newly incorporated holding entity managed
by Axight Capital Limited (Axight), established for the sole purpose
of acquiring a minority interest in the issued share capital of the
Target. Axight is an Abu Dhabi-based private equity-focused
investment manager with over US$5 billion in assets under
management and a focus on investing in developed and developing
markets across Asia Pacific.
The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an
Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets
under management, specialising in finance and credit investment
solutions. La Trobe Financial’s core offering is Australian Real Estate
Private Credit, being the provision of residential and commercial
loans originated through independent mortgage broker channels in
Australia. It also offers:
a. an ASX-listed Private Credit Fund, providing investors with
monthly income and daily liquidity from diversified
Australian real estate private credit and US mid-market
corporate credit, and
b. a US Private Credit Fund, providing both wholesale and retail
Australian investors with access to a curated and unique set
of investment products.
The vendor, Brookfield, operates in the private equity industry,
focusing on acquiring high-quality, cash-generative businesses in
various sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver, and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical
relationship between the target or La Trobe Financial and AXDI (or
their portfolio companies) in the supply of residential and
commercial loan or alternative asset management services in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
